title: Executive Order 12806—Establishment of a Fetal Tissue Bank

flags.justification: A fairly standard administrative-implementation delegation to the Secretary of Health and Human Services, not unusually broad.
Sec. 4 requires a specific progress report to the President by December 31, 1992.
Single coherent purpose: establishing the tissue bank and its access procedures.

'Appropriate peer review process' and 'meritorious research projects' (Sec. 2) are somewhat open-textured standards without further specification, though the tissue source itself is precisely defined.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
No review or reauthorization mechanism exists for the underlying program beyond the single specified progress report; the program itself has no defined review point.
Sec. 3 explicitly requires consistency with 'all other applicable legal requirements related to fetal tissue,' deferring to rather than displacing existing legal frameworks.
The required peer review process (Sec. 2) provides a real, if not fully detailed, external scientific check on research access.

Establishes new federal biomedical research infrastructure with a specific, deliberately bounded scope — tissue is drawn exclusively from ectopic pregnancies and spontaneous abortions, a textually explicit limitation excluding induced abortions.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
The order's tissue-source limitation defines the program's scope from the outset rather than creating an exemption from a broader mandate.
